User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the SMSWIN Lottery app.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the user.

balance.description: The current balance of the wallet.

currency.description: The currency of the wallet (e.g., INR).

LotteryNumber.title: LotteryNumber
LotteryNumber.description: Represents a lottery number or ticket purchased by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lottery number.

number.description: The lottery number or a unique ticket identifier.

purchaseDate.description: The date the lottery number was purchased.

unitPrice.description: The price of one unit of the lottery number at time of purchase.

unitsPurchased.description: The number of units of the lottery number purchased.

LotteryEvent.title: LotteryEvent
LotteryEvent.description: Represents a lottery event (e.g., daily draw).

id.description: Unique identifier for the lottery event.

name.description: The name of the lottery event (e.g., 'Daily Draw').

eventDate.description: The date of the lottery event.

result.description: The winning number for the lottery event.

status.description: The status of the lottery event (e.g., 'Open', 'Closed', 'Completed').

isEnabled.description: A flag indicating if the lottery event is enabled or disabled for purchases.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit for a number in this lottery event.

prize.description: The prize for special events like LuckyDraw.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a transaction in the user's wallet.

amount.description: The amount of the transaction (positive for deposit, negative for withdrawal).

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Represents a global announcement from an admin.

content.description: The main content of the announcement.

createdAt.description: The date the announcement was created.

AppSettings.title: Application Settings
AppSettings.description: Stores global configuration for the application, such as prize amounts.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Accessible only by the user with matching userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores user wallets. Accessible only by the user with matching userId. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: walletId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user's wallet.

definition.description: Stores lottery event data. Publicly readable. Write access restricted to admins.

params.name: lotteryEventId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lottery event.

definition.description: Stores lottery numbers purchased by users. Accessible only by the user with matching userId.  Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: lotteryNumberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lottery number.

definition.description: Stores transaction history for user wallets. Accessible only by the user with matching userId. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'walletId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: walletId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user's wallet.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transaction.

definition.description: Stores global announcements. Publicly readable. Write access restricted to admins.

params.name: announcementId
params.description: The unique identifier of the announcement.

definition.description: Stores global application settings. Write access is restricted to admins. Should only contain one document with a known ID (e.g., 'app').

params.name: settingsId
params.description: The unique identifier for the settings document, typically 'app'.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to provide a secure, scalable, and debuggable solution for the SMSWIN Lottery app. It prioritizes authorization independence by avoiding hierarchical dependencies and denormalizing authorization data. The structure is segregated to ensure homogeneous security postures within collections, and access is modeled consistently using path-based ownership for user-specific data.

*   **Users Collection:** Stores user profiles. Access is restricted to the user themselves (path-based ownership).
*   **Wallets Collection:** Stores user wallets, located under each user document for path-based ownership.
*   **LotteryEvents Collection:** Stores global lottery event data.  Publicly readable, but write access restricted to admins.
*   **LotteryNumbers Collection:** Stores lottery numbers purchased by users, located under each user document for path-based ownership. This facilitates easy retrieval of a user's purchased numbers.
*   **Transactions Collection:** Stores transaction history for user wallets, located under each wallet document to provide proper separation of concerns, continuing the existing ownership hierarchy.
*   **Announcements Collection:** Stores global announcements. Publicly readable, write access restricted to admins.
*   **Settings Collection:** A new singleton collection to store global, configurable application settings like prize structures, making them manageable by admins without code changes.

This structure ensures Authorization Independence by relying solely on `request.auth.uid` and avoiding `get()` calls in security rules.  It also supports the required QAPs by:

*   **Path-Based Ownership:** Enforces clear ownership rules, making `list` operations secure and efficient, as users can only access their own data.
*   **Segregation:** Separates user-specific data from global and admin-only data, allowing for different security rules to be applied to each.

The use of hierarchical paths for user-owned data (e.g., `/users/{userId}/wallets/{walletId}`) ensures strong ownership-based security. Denormalization is employed by potentially including user id fields in nested collections where necessary to avoid complex `get()` calls in security rules.
